title: acciddasuite
text: acciddasuite package provides tools building infectious disease forecasts relies fable framework. vignette demonstrates basic example generating evaluating forecasts following standard forecasting workflow described Hyndman & Athanasopoulos (2021).

title: acciddasuite
text: like load surveillance, can follow steps formatting. demonstration purposes, load surveillance data CDC National Health Safety Network. get_data() function provides convenient interface access data using epidatr package. look df looks like, can access example csv file : example_data.csv.
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(pipetime) library(acciddasuite) df <- get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_values = "ny") head(df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   as_of      location target            target_end_date observation #>   <date>     <chr>    <chr>             <date>                <dbl> #> 1 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #> 2 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15              490 #> 3 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22              844 #> 4 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29              483 #> 5 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05              479 #> 6 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-12              573

title: acciddasuite
text: evaluate model performance, employ time series cross-validation. fit models using data available specific cutoff point (eval_start_date), forecast h weeks ahead expanding windows. back time eval_start_date , computationally intensive evaluation step . Default models :  * SNAIVE (Seasonal Naïve): Assumes week look like week last year. simplest possible baseline. * ETS (Exponential Smoothing): weighted average recent weeks matter older ones. Adapts trends seasonal patterns. * THETA: Splits data long-term trend short-term fluctuations, forecasts separately, combines . * ARIMA: Learns repeating patterns past values predict future ones. Auto-configured find best fit. Visualize forecasts accessing plot element forecast object:
code: # We ony evaluate on the last 30 days of data for demonstration purposes eval_start_date <- max(df$target_end_date) - 30 fcast = get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4, # Select top 4 models   h = 4 # forecast 4 weeks ahead ) |>   time_pipe("base fcast", log = "timing")  fcast #> <accidda_cast> #>  #> Models evaluated: #>  model_id       wis #>    <char>     <num> #>     THETA  26.88197 #>     ARIMA  29.14217 #>       ETS  31.33395 #>  ENSEMBLE  52.64632 #>    SNAIVE 256.25401 #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2026-02-07  #>   To:   2026-04-04  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hubcast   hub forecast object #>   $score     model ranking table #>   $plot      ggplot2 object fcast$plot

title: acciddasuite
text: Additonal models can added defining list passing get_fcast(). models compatible fable framework (see fable documentation information). can check long step took calling pipetime::get_log():
code: library(fable) library(fable.prophet) extra <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1,1,0)),   PROPHET = prophet(observation ~ season("year")),   EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3, std_si = 2, rt_window = 7) )  fcast = get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = eval_start_date,   top_n = 4, # Select top 4 models   h = 3, # forecast 3 weeks ahead,   extra_models = extra ) |>   time_pipe("extra fcast", log = "timing") get_log() #> $timing #>             timestamp       label duration unit #> 1 2026-03-18 14:24:26  base fcast 2.417592 secs #> 2 2026-03-18 14:24:29 extra fcast 2.867576 secs

title: acciddasuite
text: RespiLens platform sharing visualizing respiratory disease forecasts. submit forecasts RespiLens, can use to_respilens() save file JSON format upload MyRespiLens .

title: Preparing External Data
text: acciddasuite supports forecasting hospitalization incidence due COVID-19, RSV, influenza based given ground truth data. able quickly pull state-level inc hosp data using get_data(), may desire use hospitalization ground truth data forecasting (.e., df parameter modeling get_fcast()). , must esnure data adheres format described . Please note get_fcast() processes one location time; multiple locations’ ground truth data, separate ground truth dataset one data frame per location initiate get_fcast() run .

title: Preparing External Data
text: ground truth data must contain 4 distinct columns:

title: Preparing External Data
text: data target_end_date, observation, location target columns, must ensure following successful processing get_fcast(): target_end_date values unique NAs (.e., duplicate missing values target_end_date column) target_end_date values data must weekly cadence (.e., target_end_date values exactly one week apart, continuous week 1 end) target column must contain one unique value (.e, target recorded data) dataset contain ≥ 52 entries (weeks observations; rows) target_end_date < eval_start_date parameter pass get_fcast(). Please note NA entries observation column filtered silently, may result reduction number observations dataset. optimal ensure NA values resolved prior use get_fcast(). ensuring compliance stipulations , can read dataset (either data.frame tibble) pass df parameter get_fcast().

title: Preparing External Data
text: Properly converted data, example, df may resemble : Data type compliance: can now used df parameter get_fcast(): eval_start_date (2025-01-01) begins 52 consecutive weeks target_end_date entries: can 52 entries preceding eval_start_date, 52 minimum.
code: ##   target_end_date observation location             target ## 1      2024-01-01          13       NY inc hosp influenza ## 2      2024-01-08          15       NY inc hosp influenza ## 3      2024-01-15          19       NY inc hosp influenza ## 4      2024-01-22          22       NY inc hosp influenza ## 5      2024-01-29          25       NY inc hosp influenza ## 6      2024-02-05          11       NY inc hosp influenza class(df$target_end_date) ## [1] "Date" class(df$observation) ## [1] "integer" class(df$location) ## [1] "character" class(df$target) ## [1] "character" x = get_fcast(     df,     eval_start_date = "2025-01-01",     h = 3,     top_n = 5 ) sum(df$target_end_date < eval_start_date, na.rm = TRUE) ## [1] 52

title: Disease Forecast Planning for Public Health
text: Disease forecasting important tool public health, however, maximize utility tools, thoughtful planning required clearly define approach problem needing addressed. ACCIDDA Forecast Suite provides comprehensive toolkit building infectious disease forecasting pipelines hubs, discussions planning prior starting forecasting project important. provide series questions consider addressing prior using package. package suite still active development, welcome contributions feedback community.

what: Step 1: Why are we interested in forecasting?

Forecasts provide range possible trajectories time period. Predicting specific number, number total hospitalizations time period require different approach models. title: A suite of tools for epidemic forecasting
text: Save myRespiLens format:
code: library(acciddasuite) df <- get_data(pathogen = "flu", geo_values = "ny") fcast <- df |>    get_fcast(eval_start_date = max(df$target_end_date) - 30) fcast #> <accidda_cast> #>  #> Models evaluated: #>  model_id        wis #>    <char>      <num> #>     ARIMA   85.65125 #>       ETS  120.98738 #>  ENSEMBLE  128.35163 #>     THETA  214.99363 #>    SNAIVE 1351.92228 #>  #> Forecast horizon: #>   From: 2026-01-31  #>   To:   2026-03-28  #>  #> Contents: #>   $hubcast   hub forecast object #>   $score     model ranking table #>   $plot      ggplot2 object fcast$plot to_respilens(fcast, path = "example_respilens.json")

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: Estimates current reproduction number (Rt) using EpiEstim simulates future incidence using projections. Works regular aggregation period integrates fable workflow via model().

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
code: EPIESTIM(   formula,   mean_si,   std_si,   rt_window = 14L,   n_sim = 100L,   R_fix_within = TRUE )

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: formula Response variable, e.g. observation. Exogenous regressors supported. mean_si Mean serial interval days. std_si Standard deviation serial interval days. rt_window Sliding window width days Rt estimation (dt_out EpiEstim). Controls many recent days inform current Rt. Smaller values track recent trends closely; larger values give smoother estimate. Defaults 14 days (~2 weeks). n_sim Number simulation paths forecast distribution. R_fix_within TRUE, Rt held constant within simulated path (recommended short horizons).

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: model definition use inside model().

title: EpiEstim model for fable — EPIESTIM
text: aggregation period detected automatically tsibble index. recent data (rt_window estimation period plus serial interval period) passed EpiEstim's expectation-maximisation (EM) algorithm, reconstructs daily incidence aggregated counts estimating Rt rolling rt_window-day windows. recent Rt estimate used project forward via stochastic simulation. Forecasts returned sample distributions (one per horizon period) drawn n_sim epidemic paths.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: Fetch recent confirmed US hospital admissions NHSN source COVID-19, influenza, RSV using pub_covidcast.

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: pathogen Character. One "covid", "flu" "rsv". geo_values Character vector. Geographic values per pub_covidcast filter . Defaults "*" (states).

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
text: tibble hubverse format columns: as_of: Date data issued location: State code target: Target description (e.g., "wk inc covid hosp") target_end_date: End date target week observation: Observed hospital admissions

title: Get hospitalisation data — get_data
code: get_data(pathogen = "covid", geo_values = c("ca", "ny")) #> Warning: No API key found. You will be limited to non-complex queries and encounter rate #> limits if you proceed. #> ℹ See `?save_api_key()` for details on obtaining and setting API keys. #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> # A tibble: 584 × 5 #>    as_of      location target            target_end_date observation #>    <date>     <chr>    <chr>             <date>                <dbl> #>  1 2026-03-08 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08             4836 #>  2 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-08              517 #>  3 2026-03-08 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15             4273 #>  4 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-15              490 #>  5 2026-03-08 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22             3498 #>  6 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-22              844 #>  7 2026-03-08 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29             3100 #>  8 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-08-29              483 #>  9 2026-03-08 CA       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05             2888 #> 10 2026-03-08 NY       wk inc covid hosp 2020-09-05              479 #> # ℹ 574 more rows

what: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: Fits multiple time series models weekly incidence data evaluates short term predictive performance using expanding window rolling origin scheme.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
code: get_fcast(df, eval_start_date, h = 4, top_n = 3, extra_models = NULL)

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: df Data frame weekly observations containing target_end_date (Date), location (character), target (character), observation (numeric). eval_start_date Date string coercible Date. First date forecasts evaluated. least 52 weeks data must precede date. h Integer. Forecast horizon weeks. Default 4. top_n Integer. Number top ranked models included ensemble. Default 3. extra_models Named list additional forecasting models passed fabletools::model(). element must reference observation column compatible fabletools::forecast(). Custom models can constructed using fable modelling framework, see fabletools extension_models vignette. name list element used model label output.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: object class accidda_cast containing: forecast Final h week ahead forecasts models ensemble. score Model ranking based rolling origin WIS. plot ggplot object showing forecasts prediction intervals.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
text: eval_start_date onwards, models repeatedly refitted data available evaluation time point used forecast next h weeks. Models ranked mean WIS score across evaluation periods. best performing top_n models combined equal weight ensemble. final h week ahead forecast produced refitting selected models using full dataset.

title: Rolling origin epidemic forecasts and model evaluation — get_fcast
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ extra_models <- list(   CUSTOM_ARIMA = ARIMA(observation ~ pdq(1, 1, 0)),   PROPHET = fable.prophet::PROPHET(observation),   EPIESTIM = EPIESTIM(observation, mean_si = 3.96, std_si = 4.75) )  get_fcast(   df,   eval_start_date = "2025-01-01",   h = 4,   top_n = 3,   extra_models = extra_models ) } # }
